policy_crossover: MOM median-wage trends intersect with AIScope exposure: 11.8% of occupations sit in the high-wage/high-exposure quadrant while 20.0% combine lower wages with high exposure — a structural tension for SkillsFuture and sector councils as 2026 reasoning-heavy automation compresses premium desk workflows faster than frontline PWM floors adjust.
market_overview: Singapore snapshot (570 SSOC-style rows): mean AI exposure sits near 5.50/10 with a fat right tail in clerical and professional services as 2026 reasoning models absorb cross-document work. PWM-covered frontline roles remain capped but face gradual hardware substitution in pilots; policy focus should pair wage statistics with reskilling velocity, not headline model releases alone.
